mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate decline observations, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that sustains corporate revenue trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 52%, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly contractions in industrial production reveal supply-side bottlenecks that force corporate margin compression, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of contraction periods, or the mechanism reverses direction during liquidity provision phases.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rate normalization that stabilizes equity discount factors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL annualized growth by > 1.0%
failure_condition: The real rate spread fails to predict positive 20-day returns when inflation expectations remain anchored above 3.0%, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate consumption capacity, depressing corporate earnings forecasts and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show positive directionality in > 50% of payroll decline months, or the signal exhibits zero predictive accuracy during structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized confirms robust economic expansion that validates equity risk premiums, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of high-GDP-release instances, or the signal loses significance when accompanied by rising term premium spreads.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and stable employment indicates inventory accumulation and impending capacity cuts, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months while PAYEMS remains flat
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative 20-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 53%, or the mechanism breaks down during supply chain normalization phases.
